questions.q: By what popular epithet is the Tamil cinema actor Rajinikanth widely known?
questions.explanation: Rajinikanth is universally referred to as 'Superstar', a title strongly associated with him in Tamil cinema.

questions.q: In which Indian film industry did Rajinikanth become the biggest mass icon?
questions.explanation: Rajinikanth rose to become Tamil cinema's biggest mass icon, though he has acted across several Indian languages.

questions.q: Which 1975 film directed by K. Balachander marked Rajinikanth's film debut?
questions.explanation: Rajinikanth made his film debut in K. Balachander's 1975 Tamil film Apoorva Raagangal.

questions.q: What humble job did Rajinikanth famously do before becoming a film actor?
questions.explanation: Before entering films, Rajinikanth worked as a bus conductor with the Bangalore Transport Service.

questions.q: In which year was Rajinikanth born?
questions.explanation: Rajinikanth was born on 12 December 1950 in Bangalore.

questions.q: In which 1995 film did Rajinikanth play the title role of a reformed don turned auto driver?
questions.explanation: In the 1995 blockbuster Baashha, Rajinikanth played an auto driver hiding a past as a Mumbai don.

questions.q: Which 1995 Rajinikanth film became a major hit in Japan, boosting his popularity there?
questions.explanation: Muthu (1995) was dubbed into Japanese and became hugely popular in Japan, expanding Rajinikanth's overseas fame.

questions.q: In which 2010 science-fiction film directed by S. Shankar did Rajinikanth play a scientist and an android?
questions.explanation: In Enthiran (2010), Rajinikanth played both Dr. Vaseegaran and the android Chitti.

questions.q: Rajinikanth is especially celebrated in his films for what kind of on-screen traits?
questions.explanation: Rajinikanth is famous for his stylised mannerisms, signature gestures and punch dialogues that thrill fans.

questions.q: Which national civilian honour did Rajinikanth receive in 2016 for his contributions to the arts?
questions.explanation: Rajinikanth was awarded the Padma Vibhushan in 2016; he had earlier received the Padma Bhushan in 2000.

questions.q: What is Rajinikanth's birth name, given before he adopted his screen name?
questions.explanation: Rajinikanth was born Shivaji Rao Gaekwad and took the name Rajinikanth at the start of his film career.

questions.q: In K. Balachander's 1976 film Moondru Mudichu, what kind of role did Rajinikanth play?
questions.explanation: In Moondru Mudichu (1976) Rajinikanth played a callous negative character, one of the antagonist roles that defined his early career.

questions.q: In which Indian state was Rajinikanth born and raised before entering Tamil cinema?
questions.explanation: Rajinikanth was born in Bangalore, in the state of Karnataka, into a Marathi-speaking family.

questions.q: At which institution did Rajinikanth train in acting before his film debut?
options: Film and Television Institute of India, Pune
questions.explanation: Rajinikanth studied acting at the Madras Film Institute (the Adyar Film Institute) in Chennai.

questions.q: Which composer scored the music for Rajinikanth's films Muthu and Enthiran?
questions.explanation: A. R. Rahman composed the music for both Muthu (1995) and Enthiran (2010).

questions.q: Which leading lady was paired opposite Rajinikanth in the hits Arunachalam (1997) and Padayappa (1999)?
questions.explanation: Soundarya starred opposite Rajinikanth in several hits, including Arunachalam (1997) and Padayappa (1999).

questions.q: In which 1999 film directed by K. S. Ravikumar did Rajinikanth play a character who triumphs over a domineering woman?
questions.explanation: In Padayappa (1999), Rajinikanth's character clashes with and ultimately overcomes the proud Neelambari.

questions.q: Which actress played the antagonist Neelambari opposite Rajinikanth in the 1999 film Padayappa?
questions.explanation: Ramya Krishnan played the memorable villain Neelambari in Padayappa (1999).

questions.q: In which 2007 S. Shankar film, then one of India's most expensive, did Rajinikanth star as an NRI fighting corruption?
questions.explanation: Sivaji: The Boss (2007), directed by S. Shankar, featured Rajinikanth as a software engineer battling corruption.

questions.q: Rajinikanth appeared alongside Amitabh Bachchan in which 1983 Hindi film directed by T. Rama Rao?
questions.explanation: Rajinikanth starred in the Hindi film Andhaa Kaanoon (1983) alongside Amitabh Bachchan, marking a key Bollywood role.

questions.q: In the 2005 film Chandramukhi, Rajinikanth played a psychiatrist confronting which kind of central mystery?
questions.explanation: In Chandramukhi (2005), Rajinikanth's psychiatrist character investigates a supposed haunting linked to a split personality.

questions.q: Rajinikanth's daughter Aishwarya directed which 2014 film starring her father?
questions.explanation: Aishwarya Rajinikanth directed the motion-capture animated film Kochadaiiyaan (2014), starring Rajinikanth.

questions.q: In the 1978 Tamil film Bairavi, Rajinikanth was first promoted with which title that became his lifelong tag?
questions.explanation: The 1978 film Bairavi is credited with first marketing Rajinikanth as 'Superstar', a title that stuck.

questions.q: Which 1980 film, a Tamil remake of a Hindi hit, was an early solo-hero blockbuster that helped establish Rajinikanth as a lead star?
questions.explanation: Billa (1980), a remake of the Hindi film Don, was a major hit that cemented Rajinikanth's status as a solo lead.

questions.q: Rajinikanth received the Centenary Award for Indian Film Personality of the Year at the 2019 International Film Festival of India in which city?
questions.explanation: The award was presented at the 50th International Film Festival of India held in Panaji, Goa, in 2019.

questions.q: In 2021 Rajinikanth was honoured with which prestigious lifetime-achievement award of Indian cinema?
options: National Film Award for Best Actor
questions.explanation: Rajinikanth was conferred the Dadasaheb Phalke Award (for 2019) in 2021, Indian cinema's highest honour.

questions.q: Which 2002 Rajinikanth film, directed by Suresh Krissna and carrying a spiritual theme, underperformed at the box office?
questions.explanation: Baba (2002), written by Rajinikanth himself, was a commercial disappointment despite high expectations.

questions.q: Rajinikanth's 2016 film Kabali, in which he played an aging gangster, was directed by whom?
questions.explanation: Kabali (2016) was directed by Pa. Ranjith, who also directed Rajinikanth in Kaala (2018).

questions.q: The 2018 film Kaala, starring Rajinikanth as a Dharavi don, was set primarily in which city?
questions.explanation: In Kaala (2018), Rajinikanth played a slum leader fighting for land rights in the Dharavi area of Mumbai.

questions.q: Rajinikanth and his wife Latha Rangachari have two daughters; which of their daughters married actor Dhanush?
questions.explanation: Rajinikanth's elder daughter Aishwarya married actor Dhanush in 2004, while younger daughter Soundarya is a filmmaker.
